title: DFD Level 0 & Level 1 — Hospital Management System
examTip: When drawing the Hospital DFD, ensure you capture the patient's symptoms/details going in and diagnostic prescriptions coming out, alongside the doctor's inputs. Data stores should reflect distinct categories of persistent data, such as Patient Records, Appointments, Medical History, and Billing Ledger. Remember, all data flows must have descriptive labels.
theory:
A Data Flow Diagram (DFD) for a Hospital Management System (HMS) outlines how patient information, medical histories, scheduling requests, and billing records move through various departments. DFD modeling enables administrators and database developers to chart data structures and identify critical system boundaries.

Level 0 Context Diagram: Defines the interaction between the system and three main external entities: Patient, Doctor, and Hospital Administrator. The Patient registers with symptoms and demographic details and receives prescriptions, appointment schedules, and invoices. The Doctor enters diagnosis notes and treatment schemes and retrieves patient histories. The Administrator inputs doctor schedules, configures system roles, and receives operational and financial report summaries.

Level 1 Detail DFD: Decomposes the hospital operations into four key processes supported by database tables (data stores):
1. 1.1 Patient Registration: Captures demographic details from the patient and saves them to 'D1: Patient Records Database'.
2. 1.2 Appointment Scheduling: Queries the 'D2: Appointments Database' to check physician availability, assigns open time slots, and sends confirmations.
3. 1.3 Clinical Management: Used by doctors to document diagnostic details, treatment protocols, and prescription orders in 'D3: Medical History Database'.
4. 1.4 Billing & Invoice Processing: Collects medication lists and consultation durations from 'D3: Medical History Database', retrieves patient status from 'D1', calculates final fees, posts them to 'D4: Billing Ledger', and outputs invoices to the Patient and financial reports to the Administrator.
keyPoints: Level 0 outlines the data bounds between the Patient, Doctor, Administrator, and the central system
Level 1 separates clinical care (diagnosis, prescriptions) from administrative tasks (registration, scheduling, billing)
D1: Patient Records Database persists demographics, while D3: Medical History Database logs diagnoses
D2: Appointments Database stores doctor timetables and booked slots to prevent scheduling conflicts
D4: Billing Ledger records treatment costs, invoice statuses, and patient payment transactions
useCases: Designing logical data architecture for integrated hospital information networks (HIS)
Documenting electronic health record (EHR) data workflows for healthcare regulatory compliance
Optimizing outpatient scheduling systems and billing cycles in private clinics and public hospitals
mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
  subgraph L0["Level 0: Context DFD"]
    direction LR
    PatientL0["Patient"]:::dfdEntity
    HMS["1.0 Hospital Management System"]:::dfdProcess
    DoctorL0["Doctor"]:::dfdEntity
    AdminL0["Hospital Administrator"]:::dfdEntity
    
    PatientL0 -->|"Registration & Symptoms"| HMS
    HMS -->|"Appointments & Prescriptions"| PatientL0
    DoctorL0 -->|"Treatment Plans & Reports"| HMS
    HMS -->|"Patient Medical History"| DoctorL0
    AdminL0 -->|"Staff Info & System Configuration"| HMS
    HMS -->|"Operational & Financial Reports"| AdminL0
  end

  subgraph L1["Level 1: Detail DFD"]
    direction TB
    PatientL1["Patient"]:::dfdEntity
    DoctorL1["Doctor"]:::dfdEntity
    AdminL1["Hospital Administrator"]:::dfdEntity
    
    P1["1.1 Patient Registration"]:::dfdProcess
    P2["1.2 Appointment Scheduling"]:::dfdProcess
    P3["1.3 Clinical Management"]:::dfdProcess
    P4["1.4 Billing & Invoice Processing"]:::dfdProcess
    
    D1[("D1: Patient Records DB")]:::dfdDataStore
    D2[("D2: Appointments DB")]:::dfdDataStore
    D3[("D3: Medical History DB")]:::dfdDataStore
    D4[("D4: Billing Ledger")]:::dfdDataStore
    
    %% Patient registration
    PatientL1 -->|"Personal Details"| P1
    P1 -->|"Save New Patient"| D1
    P1 -->|"Patient ID Slip"| PatientL1
    
    %% Appointments scheduling
    PatientL1 -->|"Request Appointment"| P2
    P2 -->|"Check Doctor Availability"| D2
    P2 -->|"Book Slot"| D2
    P2 -->|"Confirmation Slip"| PatientL1
    DoctorL1 -->|"Retrieve Schedule"| P2
    
    %% Clinical management
    DoctorL1 -->|"Enter Diagnosis & Prescription"| P3
    P3 -->|"Fetch Patient Record"| D1
    P3 -->|"Write Treatment Log"| D3
    D3 -->|"Read Medical History"| P3
    P3 -->|"Treatment Details"| PatientL1

    %% Billing & Invoicing
    P4 -->|"Read Treatment Costs"| D3
    P4 -->|"Read Patient Demographics"| D1
    P4 -->|"Post Invoice Balance"| D4
    PatientL1 -->|"Submit Payment"| P4
    P4 -->|"Payment Receipt"| PatientL1
    P4 -->|"Financial Statements"| AdminL1
  end
